questions.question: What is a triangle?
questions.answer: A triangle is a polygon with three sides and three angles.

questions.question: What are the types of triangles based on sides?
questions.answer: Triangles based on sides are: equilateral (all sides equal), isosceles (two sides equal), and scalene (all sides unequal).

questions.question: What are the types of triangles based on angles?
questions.answer: Triangles based on angles are: acute-angled (all angles < 90°), right-angled (one angle = 90°), and obtuse-angled (one angle > 90°).

questions.question: State the sum of angles in a triangle.
questions.answer: The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is 180°.

questions.question: What is a right-angled triangle?
questions.answer: A triangle in which one of the angles is 90° is called a right-angled triangle.

questions.question: Define an equilateral triangle.
questions.answer: A triangle in which all three sides are equal is called an equilateral triangle.

questions.question: Define an isosceles triangle.
questions.answer: A triangle in which two sides are equal is called an isosceles triangle.

questions.question: Define a scalene triangle.
questions.answer: A triangle in which all three sides are unequal is called a scalene triangle.

questions.question: What is the perimeter of a triangle?
questions.answer: The perimeter of a triangle is the sum of the lengths of its three sides.

questions.question: What is the altitude of a triangle?
questions.answer: The altitude of a triangle is the perpendicular distance from a vertex to the opposite side or its extension.

questions.question: What is a median of a triangle?
questions.answer: A median of a triangle is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

questions.question: What is a centroid of a triangle?
questions.answer: The point of intersection of the medians of a triangle is called the centroid.

questions.question: What is an angle bisector of a triangle?
questions.answer: An angle bisector of a triangle is a line that divides an angle into two equal angles.

questions.question: What is the incentre of a triangle?
questions.answer: The point where the angle bisectors of a triangle intersect is called the incentre.

questions.question: What is the circumcentre of a triangle?
questions.answer: The point where the perpendicular bisectors of the sides of a triangle intersect is called the circumcentre.

questions.question: What is the orthocentre of a triangle?
questions.answer: The point where the altitudes of a triangle intersect is called the orthocentre.

questions.question: Define similar triangles.
questions.answer: Two triangles are similar if their corresponding angles are equal and corresponding sides are proportional.

questions.question: State the criteria for similarity of triangles.
questions.answer: Triangles are similar if: (i) AAA (all angles equal), (ii) SAS (two sides proportional and included angle equal), or (iii) SSS (all sides proportional).

questions.question: What is the Pythagoras theorem?
questions.answer: In a right-angled triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

questions.question: Define the hypotenuse of a right-angled triangle.
questions.answer: The hypotenuse is the side opposite the right angle in a right-angled triangle.

questions.question: Define an exterior angle of a triangle.
questions.answer: An exterior angle is formed when a side of a triangle is extended. It equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles.

questions.question: State the mid-point theorem.
questions.answer: The line joining the midpoints of two sides of a triangle is parallel to the third side and half of it.

questions.question: Define altitude, median, and angle bisector.
questions.answer: Altitude: perpendicular from vertex to opposite side, Median: vertex to midpoint of opposite side, Angle bisector: divides angle into two equal angles.

questions.question: What is the relation between sides and angles in a triangle?
questions.answer: In a triangle, the larger side lies opposite the larger angle and vice versa.

questions.question: What is the sum of exterior angles of a triangle?
questions.answer: The sum of the exterior angles of a triangle, one at each vertex, is 360°.
